what_it_does: This file acts as a README for the _qa/ directory, documenting that it holds throwaway QA artifacts (screenshots and responsive-harness.html) and is not part of the shipped product.
observations: The _qa/ folder contains screenshots and a responsive-harness.html file used during app verification.
Nothing in _qa/ is loaded by Counterpart Studio.html, so the folder is safe to ignore or delete.
responsive-harness.html embeds the app in 390px and 768px iframes to exercise the real responsive breakpoints, because the preview pane is fixed-width.
The file explicitly warns not to wire any app code to anything inside _qa/.
standouts: Explicit isolation disclaimer: the entire folder is declared non-product and off-limits to app wiring.
The responsive harness is described as a reusable tool for future responsive checks using real viewport widths.
It distinguishes the harness's real breakpoints from the fixed-width preview pane.
conventions: Follows the AGENTS.md README convention for a subdirectory.
States scope boundaries clearly and warns against coupling app code to QA artifacts.
Uses plain, directive language ('Safe to ignore or delete', 'Don't wire any app code').
concerns: The contents are called 'throwaway' yet the responsive harness is recommended for reuse, creating a slight purpose tension.
No details are given on how to run the harness or where screenshots are stored/organized.
notes: This is a scope-boundary README: it tells readers the _qa/ folder is disposable QA scaffolding and should not be integrated into the app.
questions: Where exactly is responsive-harness.html located within _qa/ and how is it opened?
Which specific responsive breakpoints does the harness verify beyond the two viewport widths mentioned?
Are screenshots organized by test case, date, or viewport size?
How is the harness kept in sync if the app's preview pane or breakpoints change?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (document the _qa/ directory and its non-product artifacts) matches what the file actually does.
apparent_themes: Separation of QA scaffolding from product code
Defensive isolation to prevent accidental coupling
intention_signals: It is trying to mark the _qa/ directory as a scratch verification area, keep its artifacts from being mistaken as product code, and preserve a reusable responsive-test harness for future checks.
novelty: It is unusually defensive in explicitly declaring an entire directory as non-product and forbidding app wiring to its contents.
contribution: It contributes a clear boundary statement for the _qa/ directory and a concise description of the responsive harness as a real-breakpoint verification tool that can be reused independently of the fixed-width preview pane.
summary_for_embedding: AGENTS.md in the _qa directory documents that the folder contains throwaway QA artifacts (screenshots and a responsive-harness.html) and is not loaded by the Counterpart Studio app.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to set scope boundaries for QA artifacts and explain how the responsive harness verifies real breakpoints outside the fixed-width preview pane.
